title: Chandra Chalisa
intro: Shri Chandra Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Lord Chandra
description:
|| Shri Chandra Prabhu Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Sheesh nava Arihant ko, Siddhan karun pranam.
Upadhyay Acharya ka, le sukhkari naam.
Sarv sadhu aur Saraswati, jin mandir sukhkar.
Chandrapuri ke Chandra ko, man mandir mein dhaar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai jai Swami Shri Jin Chanda, tumko nirakh bhaye ananda.
Tum hi Prabhu devan ke deva, karun tumhare pad ki seva.
Vesh Digambar keh lata hai, sab jag ke man bhata hai.
Nasa par hai drishti tumhari, mohani murti kitni pyari.
Teen lok ki baatein jaano, teen kaal kshan mein pehchano.
Naam tumhara kitna pyara, bhoot pret sab kare nivara.
Tum jag mein sarvagya kehlao, ashtam Teerthankar kehlao.
Mahasen jo pita tihare, Lakshmana ke dil ke pyare.
Taj Vaijayant vimaan sidhaye, Lakshmana ke ur mein aaye.
Posh vadi ekadash nami, janm liya Chandra Prabhu Swami.
Muni Samantbhadra the Swami, unhe bhasm vyadhi bimari.
Vaishnav dharm jabhi apnaya, apne ko pandit kehlaya.
Kaha Rao se baat bataun, Mahadev ko bhog khilaun.
Pratidin uttam bhojan aave, unko munijan chipakar khave.
Isi tarah nij rog bhagaya, ban gayi kanchan jaisi kaya.
Ek ladke ne pata chalaya, foran raja ko bataya.
Tab Rajan farmaya Muni ji ko, naman karo Shivpindi ko.
Raja se tab Muni ji bole, namaskar pindi nahi jhele.
Raja ne zanjeer mangayi, us Shivpindi mein bandhvayi.
Muni ne Swayambhu paath banaya, pindi fati achambha chhaya.
Chandraprabh ki murti dikhayi, sab ne jay-jaykar manayi.
Nagar Firozabad kahaye, paas nagar Chandwar bataye.
Chandrasen Raja kehlaya, us par dushman chadhkar aaya.
Rao tumhari stuti gayi, sab faujo ko maar bhagayi.
Dushman ko maloom ho jawe, nagar gherne phir aa jawe.
Pratima Jamuna mein padharayi, nagar chhodkar parja dhayi.
Bahut samay hi beeta hai ki, ek yati ko sapna dekha.
Bade jatan se pratima payi, mandir mein lakar padharayi.
Vaishnavon ne chaal chalayi, pratima Lakshman ki batayi.
Ab toh Jaini jan ghabrawe, Chandra Prabhu ki murti batawe.
Chinh Chandrama ka bataya, tab Swami tumko tha paya.
Sonagiri mein sau mandir hai, ek badhkar ek sundar hain.
Samavasharan tha yahan par aaya, Chandra Prabhu updesh sunaya.
Chandra Prabhu ka mandir bhari, jisko puje sab nar aur nari.
Saat haath ki murti batlayi, lal rang pratima batayi.
Mandir aur bahut bataye, shobha varant paar na paaye.
Paar karo meri yeh naiyya, tum bin koi nahi khivaiya.
Prabhu main tumse kuch nahin chahun, bhav-bhav mein darshan paun.
Main hun Swami daas tumhara, karo Nath ab toh nistara.
Swami aap daya dikhao, Chandradas ko Chandra banao.

|| Doha ||
Nit chalisahin baar, paath kare chalis din.
Khey sugandh apar, Sonagir mein aay ke.
Hoye Kuber saman, janm daridri hoye jo.
Jiske nahi santan, naam vansh jag mein chale.
meaning:
|| Shri Chandra Prabhu Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Sheesh nava Arihant ko, Siddhan karun pranam.
(Bowing my head to the Arihant, I offer salutations to the Siddhas.)
Upadhyay Acharya ka, le sukhkari naam.
(Taking the auspicious names of the Upadhyaya and Acharya.)
Sarv sadhu aur Saraswati, jin mandir sukhkar.
(Remembering all sages, Goddess Saraswati, and the blissful Jina temple,)
Chandrapuri ke Chandra ko, man mandir mein dhaar.
(I enshrine the Chandra of Chandrapuri within the temple of my heart.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jai jai Swami Shri Jin Chanda, tumko nirakh bhaye ananda.
(Victory to you, O Lord Chandra Prabhu; beholding you brings joy to the mind.)
Tum hi Prabhu devan ke deva, karun tumhare pad ki seva.
(You are the Lord of the deities; I serve at your holy feet.)
Vesh Digambar keh lata hai, sab jag ke man bhata hai.
(Your Digambara (sky-clad) form is celebrated and loved by the whole world.)
Nasa par hai drishti tumhari, mohani murti kitni pyari.
(Your gaze is fixed on the tip of your nose; how lovely and enchanting your divine image is.)
Teen lok ki baatein jaano, teen kaal kshan mein pehchano.
(You know the affairs of the three worlds and perceive the three times in an instant.)
Naam tumhara kitna pyara, bhoot pret sab kare nivara.
(Your name is so dear; it dispels all evil spirits and negative forces.)
Tum jag mein sarvagya kehlao, ashtam Teerthankar kehlao.
(You are known as the Omniscient in the world and are the eighth Tirthankara.)
Mahasen jo pita tihare, Lakshmana ke dil ke pyare.
(Mahasen is your father; you are the beloved of your mother, Lakshmana.)
Taj Vaijayant vimaan sidhaye, Lakshmana ke ur mein aaye.
(Leaving the Vaijayant celestial chariot, you entered the womb of Mother Lakshmana.)
Posh vadi ekadash nami, janm liya Chandra Prabhu Swami.
(On the eleventh day of the dark fortnight of Posh, you took birth, O Lord Chandra Prabhu.)
Muni Samantbhadra the Swami, unhe bhasm vyadhi bimari.
(Muni Samantbhadra was a great sage who suffered from the leprosy/ash disease.)
Vaishnav dharm jabhi apnaya, apne ko pandit kehlaya.
(He adopted the Vaishnava guise and called himself a scholar.)
Kaha Rao se baat bataun, Mahadev ko bhog khilaun.
(He told the King that he would feed Mahadev.)
Pratidin uttam bhojan aave, unko munijan chipakar khave.
(Every day, excellent food arrived, which the sages ate in secret.)
Isi tarah nij rog bhagaya, ban gayi kanchan jaisi kaya.
(In this way, he dispelled his disease, and his body became like gold.)
Ek ladke ne pata chalaya, foran raja ko bataya.
(A boy discovered this secret and immediately informed the King.)
Tab Rajan farmaya Muni ji ko, naman karo Shivpindi ko.
(The King ordered the Muni to bow to the Shiva-Pindi.)
Raja se tab Muni ji bole, namaskar pindi nahi jhele.
(The Muni told the King, "I cannot bow, for this Pindi will not withstand the salutation.")
Raja ne zanjeer mangayi, us Shivpindi mein bandhvayi.
(The King had chains brought and bound them to the Pindi.)
Muni ne Swayambhu paath banaya, pindi fati achambha chhaya.
(The Muni recited the Swayambhu Stotra; the Pindi burst, causing great wonder.)
Chandraprabh ki murti dikhayi, sab ne jay-jaykar manayi.
(The idol of Chandra Prabhu appeared, and everyone rejoiced in victory.)
Nagar Firozabad kahaye, paas nagar Chandwar bataye.
(It is associated with Firozabad and the nearby town of Chandwar.)
Chandrasen Raja kehlaya, us par dushman chadhkar aaya.
(When King Chandrasen was attacked by an enemy,)
Rao tumhari stuti gayi, sab faujo ko maar bhagayi.
(He sang your praise, and the enemy forces were routed and fled.)
Dushman ko maloom ho jawe, nagar gherne phir aa jawe.
(When the enemy realized this, they returned to besiege the city again.)
Pratima Jamuna mein padharayi, nagar chhodkar parja dhayi.
(The idol was placed in the Yamuna River, and the people fled the city.)
Bahut samay hi beeta hai ki, ek yati ko sapna dekha.
(After much time had passed, a Yati (ascetic) had a dream.)
Bade jatan se pratima payi, mandir mein lakar padharayi.
(With great effort, the idol was found and installed in the temple.)
Vaishnavon ne chaal chalayi, pratima Lakshman ki batayi.
(The Vaishnavas played a trick and claimed the idol was of Lakshman.)
Ab toh Jaini jan ghabrawe, Chandra Prabhu ki murti batawe.
(The Jains were worried, but proved it was the idol of Chandra Prabhu.)
Chinh Chandrama ka bataya, tab Swami tumko tha paya.
(The symbol of the Moon confirmed that it was you, O Lord.)
Sonagiri mein sau mandir hai, ek badhkar ek sundar hain.
(In Sonagiri there are a hundred temples, each one more beautiful than the other.)
Samavasharan tha yahan par aaya, Chandra Prabhu updesh sunaya.
(The Samavasarana came here, and Chandra Prabhu delivered his sermon.)
Chandra Prabhu ka mandir bhari, jisko puje sab nar aur nari.
(The temple of Chandra Prabhu is magnificent, worshipped by all men and women.)
Saat haath ki murti batlayi, lal rang pratima batayi.
(The idol is seven hands tall and of a red hue.)
Mandir aur bahut bataye, shobha varant paar na paaye.
(Many other temples exist, whose beauty is beyond description.)
Paar karo meri yeh naiyya, tum bin koi nahi khivaiya.
(Carry my boat of life across; without you, there is no one to steer it.)
Prabhu main tumse kuch nahin chahun, bhav-bhav mein darshan paun.
(O Lord, I desire nothing from you; may I have your vision in every life.)
Main hun Swami daas tumhara, karo Nath ab toh nistara.
(I am your servant, O Lord; please grant me liberation now.)
Swami aap daya dikhao, Chandradas ko Chandra banao.
(O Lord, show your mercy; make this devotee, Chandradas, like you, O Moon (Chandra).)

|| Doha ||
Nit chalisahin baar, paath kare chalis din.
(Whoever recites this forty times, for forty days,)
Khey sugandh apar, Sonagir mein aay ke.
(And offers limitless fragrance after coming to Sonagiri,)
Hoye Kuber saman, janm daridri hoye jo.
(Even the poverty-stricken becomes equal to Kubera.)
Jiske nahi santan, naam vansh jag mein chale.
(He who has no children shall have their family name continue in the world.)

faq.q1: Why is the Chandra Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: The Moon (Chandra) is the significator of the mind, emotions, and mother. The Chandra Chalisa is recited for mental peace, concentration, and to overcome emotional instability or mood swings.
faq.q2: What is the best day to recite it?
faq.a2: Monday is considered the most auspicious day for the worship of Chandra Dev. Additionally, reciting it on Purnima (Full Moon) is highly rewarding.
faq.q3: Does the Chandra Chalisa help with insomnia?
faq.a3: Yes, in astrology, the Moon is associated with sleep and tranquility. If someone suffers from insomnia due to mental stress, reciting it before bedtime is considered beneficial.
faq.q4: Does reciting it bring peace to the home environment?
faq.a4: Yes, regular recitation of the Chandra Chalisa brings positive energy into the home and reduces unnecessary disputes and mental stress among family members.
faq.q5: Is any special material required for it?
faq.a5: No special material is needed, but Chandra Dev favors white items. Wearing white clothes while reciting or offering milk-based sweets as 'Bhog' is considered auspicious.
